seoTitle: How to Integrate Shopify With GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Learn how to connect your Shopify store to GoHighLevel so customer and order data can flow into your CRM for marketing and automation.
intro: Connecting Shopify to GoHighLevel bridges your storefront with your CRM, letting customer and order data flow into workflows for marketing and follow-up. This guide covers setting up the integration and putting it to work.

steps.heading: Open the Shopify integration settings
steps.body: Go to the integrations section of GoHighLevel and locate the Shopify connector.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Log into your Shopify store when prompted and grant GoHighLevel permission to access your store data.

steps.heading: Choose what data to sync
steps.body: Decide whether you want customer records, order details, or both flowing from Shopify into GoHighLevel.

steps.heading: Map data into your CRM
steps.body: Confirm that synced Shopify customers and orders appear correctly as contacts and records inside GoHighLevel.

steps.heading: Build workflows around store activity
steps.body: Use triggers like a new order or abandoned cart to kick off automated follow-up sequences in GoHighLevel.

faq.question: What data syncs from Shopify to GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: Typically customer information and order details sync over, giving you a record of purchases tied to each contact in your CRM.

faq.question: Can I trigger a workflow when someone makes a purchase?
faq.answer: Yes, a new Shopify order can be used as a trigger to start a GoHighLevel workflow, such as a thank-you message or upsell sequence.

faq.question: Does this integration help with abandoned cart follow-up?
faq.answer: Yes, once connected, you can build workflows that reach out to customers who started checkout but didn't complete their purchase.

faq.question: Do I need a specific Shopify plan to connect with GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: Requirements can vary, so it's worth confirming your current Shopify plan supports the integrations and API access GoHighLevel needs.
